tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Dynamics connects forces to motion through Newton's laws. What object or system are you analyzing? What forces act on it, and in what directions? Is the object in equilibrium or accelerating?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Newton's 2nd Law: ΣF = ma (vector sum of forces equals mass times acceleration). Newton's 1st Law: if ΣF = 0, velocity is constant. Newton's 3rd Law: forces come in equal-and-opposite pairs on different objects. Weight: Fg = mg downward.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Draw a free-body diagram (FBD) showing ONLY forces on your object of interest — not forces the object exerts on others. Choose axes (often aligned with motion or incline). Resolve forces into components. Write ΣF = ma for each axis independently.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: List all forces with directions: weight, normal, tension, friction (f = μN for kinetic, f ≤ μₛN for static). Sum forces along each axis: ΣFₓ = maₓ, ΣFᵧ = maᵧ. If acceleration is zero in a direction, ΣF = 0 on that axis. Solve the system of equations.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Every force has an agent (what pushes/pulls) and a recipient (what feels the force). Identify contact vs. field forces. Which forces are relevant to the object you're analyzing?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Common forces: gravitational (Fg = mg), normal (perpendicular to surface), friction (parallel to surface, opposes motion/tendency to move), tension (along rope, same magnitude throughout ideal rope), spring (Fs = −kx).

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: For connected objects, consider whether to analyze each separately or as a system. Ropes and pulleys often mean equal tension and connected accelerations. Draw separate FBDs for each object if needed.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Write Newton's 2nd law for each object: T − f − mg sin θ = ma (example for incline). Count unknowns and equations — you need as many independent equations as unknowns. Eliminate internal forces if analyzing a system as a whole.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: A free-body diagram isolates one object and shows all external forces acting on it. What is your system boundary? What forces cross that boundary?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Rules for FBDs: one object per diagram, forces as vectors from the object (or center), label each force by type and agent. Do not include acceleration or velocity on the FBD — those go in ΣF = ma.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Place the object as a dot or box. Draw weight downward from center. Draw normal perpendicular to the contact surface. Draw friction parallel to the surface. Add tension, applied forces, or spring forces as appropriate.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: After completing the FBD, resolve each force into x and y components using sin/cos. Write ΣFₓ and ΣFᵧ. Check: does the normal balance weight on a flat surface with no vertical acceleration? Does friction oppose the direction of motion or impending motion?

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
